articles.title: GridWatch 2.1 Is Live
articles.subtitle: Faster alerts, better diagnostics, clearer event timelines
articles.excerpt: GridWatch 2.1 improves alert latency, adds richer diagnostics snapshots, and makes event timelines easier to scan.

bannerImage.alt: Engineer reviewing power system metrics on tablet

gallery.caption: New diagnostics parser handles noisy edge states better
Improved event indexing for large deployments

articles.contentMarkdown:
## What changed

We focused this release on three areas:

- Alert latency and reliability
- Diagnostics quality in degraded states
- Better readability of long event histories

## Why this matters

Faster and more reliable insights reduce downtime.

> If your team manages multiple systems, this update should significantly reduce triage time.

## Upgrade notes

1. Update your mobile app to the latest build
2. Verify edge updater connectivity
3. Trigger a quick diagnostics capture to confirm health

## Known limitations

- Some older edge images may still report sparse diagnostics fields
- Legacy event categories are auto-mapped, but labels may differ

## Next

We are working on in-app incident timelines and scheduled reports.

articles.title: Scheduled Cloud Maintenance
articles.subtitle: Short read-only window for analytics synchronization
articles.excerpt: A short maintenance window is planned this weekend. Local monitoring is unaffected.
articles.contentMarkdown:
## Maintenance window

We plan a short maintenance period on **Saturday 02:00-02:20 UTC**.

### Impact

- Cloud analytics may be delayed
- Local system monitoring remains available
- No firmware operations are planned during this window

### Recommendation

If you are running critical operations, avoid cloud-triggered update jobs during this period.
